steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, ${x}^{3} \left(7{x}^{2}-4x+9\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: Each term has at least $x^3$, and the coefficients $7$, $4$, and $9$ have no common factor greater than $1$. The greatest common factor is $x^3$.
